title: STANDARD FOR CANNED TROPICAL FRUIT SALAD CXS 99-1981 Formerly CAC/RS 99-1978. Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2017.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR CANNED TROPICAL FRUIT SALAD CXS 99-1981 Formerly CAC/RS 99-1978. Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2017.
sections.summary: Document title only: Standard for Canned Tropical Fruit Salad (CXS 99‑1981), with adoption/amendment history—not a substantive regulatory provision.

Defines canned tropical fruit salad as a heat‑processed, sealed product made from a mixture of specified basic and optional fruits (which may be fresh, frozen or canned) packed in water or other suitable liquid packing medium, optionally with nutritive sweeteners, and processed to prevent spoilage.

Defines the product: canned tropical fruit salad prepared from the basic fruits (Section 1.2(a)) with optional fruits (1.2(b)), packed in water or other suitable liquid, may contain nutritive sweeteners, and is heat‑processed before or after sealing to prevent spoilage.

Requires fruit ingredients to include each of the three basic fruit groups (plus any optional fruits), specifies that fruit shall be peeled/cored/trimmed/deseeded/pitted as appropriate, and lists acceptable styles/cuts for each fruit.

Lists the three basic fruits required in the product: pineapple (tidbits, pieces, dices, chips or crisp cut), papaya or mango (singly or combined — slices, dices or sections), and banana (slices or dices).

Provides a list of optional fruits that may be included (e.g., litchi, cashew flesh, guava, longan, oranges, grapefruit, grapes, maraschino cherries, passion fruit, jackfruit, melon, rambutan, peach, pears) and the permitted presentations (whole, segments, slices, dices, puree, pulp, etc.).

sections.section: Longan ( Euphoria longan ) (LOUR. STEUD.) - whole or broken segments.
sections.summary: Specifies longan (Euphoria longan) may be used as an optional fruit and provided whole or as broken segments.

sections.section: Oranges ( Citrus sinensis (L.) OSBECK and Citrus reticulata BLANCO) (including Mandarin ) whole segments.
sections.summary: Specifies oranges (Citrus sinensis and C. reticulata, including mandarins) are permitted as whole segments.

sections.section: Grapefruit ( Citrus paradisi MACFAD) - whole or half segments.
sections.summary: Specifies grapefruit (Citrus paradisi) may be included as whole or half segments.

sections.section: Grapes (cultivated edible species of Vitis ) - whole grapes of any seedless variety.
sections.summary: Specifies grapes (cultivated edible Vitis species) may be included whole, provided they are of any seedless variety.

sections.section: Maraschino cherries - (prepared from fruit conforming with the characteristics of Prunus avium L.)
sections.summary: Specifies maraschino cherries (prepared from fruit conforming to Prunus avium) may be included whole or halved and should be pitted.

sections.section: Passion fruit (cultivated edible species of Passiflora ) - pulp (flesh) with or without seeds.
sections.summary: Specifies passion fruit (cultivated Passiflora species) may be used as pulp (flesh) with or without seeds.

sections.section: Jack fruit ( Artocarpu integrifolia L.) - slices.
sections.summary: Specifies jackfruit (Artocarpus integrifolia) may be included as slices.

sections.section: Melon ( Cucumis melo L.) - slices, dices or balls.
sections.summary: Specifies melon (Cucumis melo) may be included as slices, dices or balls.

sections.section: Rambutan ( Nephelium lappaceum L.) - whole or broken segments.
sections.summary: Specifies rambutan (Nephelium lappaceum) may be included whole or as broken segments.

sections.section: Peach ( Prunus persica L. BATSCH) - pieces, dices or slices.
sections.summary: Specifies peach (Prunus persica) may be included as pieces, dices or slices.

sections.section: Pears ( Pyrus communis L.) - pieces, dices or slices.
sections.summary: Specifies pears (Pyrus communis) may be included as pieces, dices or slices.

Sets required drained-weight proportion ranges for basic fruits (pineapple 45–65%, papaya or mango 25–50%, banana 5–20%) and gives ranges for many optional fruits (various minima and maxima); a lot is acceptable if the sample mean proportions (excluding those in 2.1.2) fall within ranges and the number of nonconforming containers does not exceed the acceptance number of a sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

sections.section: 2.2.2 Classification of packing media when sugars are added
sections.summary: When sugars are added to fruit juices, the liquid media must be at least 14 °Brix and are classified by cut‑out strength (e.g., lightly vs heavily sweetened), and when sugars are added to water or water-plus-juice the packing media are classified by syrup strength (basic light syrup ≥14 °Brix, heavy syrup ≥18 °Brix); optional packing media (where permitted) include slightly sweetened water (around 10–<14 °Brix) and extra heavy syrup (not less than 22 °Brix).
Defines basic syrup strengths: light syrup not less than 14 °Brix and heavy syrup not less than 18 °Brix.

States that, where not prohibited by the country of sale, optional packing media may be used, including slightly sweetened water (about 10 °Brix up to less than 14 °Brix) and extra heavy syrup (not less than 22 °Brix).

Cut-out strength (Brix) for sweetened juice/syrup is determined on the sample average, and no individual container may have a Brix value below the minimum of the next lower packing-media category where applicable.

Quality criteria for canned tropical fruit salad cover colour, flavour, texture and specified defect limits and allowances; containers failing these requirements are 'defectives' and lot acceptance is determined by sampling (see 2.3.6) with an AQL of 6.5 for non-average requirements.
Canned tropical fruit salad must have a colour characteristic of the mixed processed fruit, except that slight bleaching of coloured cherries is acceptable.
Canned tropical fruit salad must have a normal flavour and odour characteristic of the particular blend of fruit.
The texture of each fruit ingredient in canned tropical fruit salad must be appropriate for that respective fruit.

Canned tropical fruit salad shall be substantially free from defects within specified limits: blemished fruit pieces max 2 pieces/100 g drained fruit, peel (when applicable) max 6.5 cm²/500 g total contents, and seeds (except passion fruit) max 2 g/500 g total.
A container is classified as a 'defective' if it fails one or more applicable quality requirements in sections 2.3.1–2.3.4.

A lot meets quality requirements when the count of 'defectives' (per 2.3.5) does not exceed the acceptance number of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5 for non-average criteria, and sample-average–based requirements are complied with.

Lists permitted additives and contaminant limits for canned tropical fruit salad, specifying erythrosine for cherries (GMP), cherry laurel oil 10 mg/kg (artificial cherries), bitter almond oil 40 mg/kg (artificial cherries), natural/synthetic flavourings (GMP), L‑ascorbic acid 700 mg/kg, citric acid (GMP), firming agents as calcium salts up to 350 mg/kg calculated as Ca singly or in combination; contaminants: tin (Sn) 250 mg/kg (as Sn) and lead (Pb) 1 mg/kg.
Erythrosine is permitted to colour cherries and its use is limited by good manufacturing practice.

Flavourings permitted include cherry laurel oil (artificially coloured cherries only, 10 mg/kg), bitter almond oil (artificially coloured cherries only, 40 mg/kg) and natural/synthetic flavourings limited by good manufacturing practice.
Cherry laurel oil (for artificially coloured cherries only) is limited to 10 mg/kg in the total product.
Bitter almond oil (for artificially coloured cherries only) is limited to 40 mg/kg in the total product.
Natural and synthetic flavourings are permitted but their use is limited by good manufacturing practice.
The antioxidant L‑ascorbic acid is permitted up to 700 mg/kg.
Citric acid is permitted as an acidifying agent and its use is limited by good manufacturing practice.
Non‑substantive/table cell entry (continuation of the 'agent' column) with no independent regulatory requirement provided.
Firming agents (calcium chloride, calcium lactate, calcium gluconate) are permitted up to a combined total of 350 mg/kg calculated as calcium, whether used singly or in combination.
Calcium chloride is permitted as a firming agent, with a limit of 350 mg/kg singly or as part of the combined calcium limit.
Calcium lactate is permitted as a firming agent and counts toward the combined calcium limit of 350 mg/kg when used singly or in combination.
Identifies calcium gluconate as an approved additive (acidity regulator) with its content expressed as calcium (Ca).
Sets maximum contaminant levels for the product: tin (Sn) 250 mg/kg (calculated as Sn) and lead (Pb) 1 mg/kg.
Specifies the maximum permitted tin (Sn) concentration in the product as 250 mg/kg, calculated as Sn.
Specifies the maximum permitted lead (Pb) concentration in the product as 1 mg/kg.

Recommends preparing and handling the product in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant codes, requires the product to be free from objectionable matter to the extent of good manufacturing practice, and mandates that when tested it contain no microorganisms, parasites, or microbial-origin substances in amounts that may represent a health hazard.

sections.section: 7.1.6 When sugars are added to water, or water and one or more fruit juices, the packing medium shall be declared as may be appropriate:
sections.summary: Requires that when sugars are added to water or water plus fruit juice(s), the packing medium be declared using appropriate terms such as 'Light syrup'/'Heavy syrup', 'Water slightly sweetened'/'slightly sweetened water', or 'Extra light syrup'/'Extra heavy syrup'.
Allows the packing medium declaration 'Light syrup' or 'Heavy syrup' when sugars are added to the packing medium.

sections.section: Water slightly sweetened or slightly sweetened water
sections.summary: Allows the packing medium declaration 'Water slightly sweetened' or 'slightly sweetened water' when sugars are added to the packing medium.

sections.section: Extra light syrup or Extra heavy syrup
sections.summary: Allows the packing medium declaration 'Extra light syrup' or 'Extra heavy syrup' when sugars are added to the packing medium.

sections.section: 7.1.7 When the packing medium contains water and one or more fruit juice(s), in which the fruit juice comprises 50 percent or more by volume of the packing medium, the packing medium shall be designated to indicate the preponderance of such fruit juice, as, for example:
sections.summary: Requires that if the packing medium contains water and one or more fruit juices where the fruit juice is 50% or more by volume, the packing medium be designated to indicate the preponderant juice (e.g. '(name of fruits) juice(s) and water').
Specifies the packing medium designation '(name of fruits) juice(s) and water' when fruit juice comprises 50% or more of the packing medium.
Heading only: List of ingredients (no substantive provisions in this section text).

sections.section: 7.2.1 A complete list of ingredients shall be declared on the label in descending order of proportion in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), 2  except as provided for in 7.2.2 and 7.2.3.
sections.summary: Requires a complete ingredient list on the label in descending order of proportion per the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), except as provided in sections 7.2.2 and 7.2.3.

sections.section: 7.2.2 The declaration of Maraschino cherries shall be:
sections.summary: Mandates that Maraschino cherries be declared on the label as 'Cherries artificially coloured with added flavourings'.
Specifies the exact ingredient declaration phrase 'Cherries artificially coloured with added flavourings' for Maraschino cherries.

sections.section: 7.2.3 If L-ascorbic acid is added to preserve colour, its presence shall be declared in the list of ingredients in the following manner:
sections.summary: Requires that if L-ascorbic acid is added to preserve colour, it be declared in the ingredient list as 'L-ascorbic acid added as an antioxidant'.
Specifies the exact ingredient declaration phrase 'L-ascorbic acid added as an antioxidant' when L-ascorbic acid is used to preserve colour.
States that methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions shall be used to check compliance.

sections.section: For checking compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) 3  relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.
sections.summary: Specifies that the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions must be used for compliance testing.
